text.title: growth-brand-positioning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for growth-brand-positioning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Target audience too broad ("businesses", "developers" without qualifiers).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Message resonates with no one; sales conversions remain low despite high traffic because wrong-fit buyers are not disqualified.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Features are easily copied within one product cycle. Position on value, approach, or outcome — things that take more than 6 months to replicate.

failure-mode.description: Claiming too many differentiators at once.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multi-pillar positioning is forgettable. Customers compress to one thing in their memory; let them compress to the thing you want them to remember.

failure-mode.description: Inside-out thinking: leading with features instead of customer problems.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Customers care about their goals and obstacles, not the product's architecture. Positioning must start from customer situation language.

failure-mode.description: Changing positioning within 12 months without a significant product or market reason.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Brand recognition requires repetition. Frequent pivots prevent any single positioning from accumulating recognition in the market.

failure-mode.description: LLMs default to category-defining puffery: "the leading platform", "the most powerful solution".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Superlatives are unverifiable and unconvincing. Force the agent to replace every superlative with a concrete proof point tied to a named metric or customer.

failure-mode.description: Agents conflate features with differentiators when generating positioning.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run a follow-up pass: "Can a competitor ship this in 6 months? If yes, it is a feature, not positioning." Differentiators must have structural defensibility.

failure-mode.description: Positioning generated without verbatim customer-language input reads as bland and inside-out.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Block synthesis until customer interview quotes are in context. Agents hallucinate target-customer pain when research inputs are absent.

failure-mode.description: Competitive analysis from web scraping captures stated positioning, not actual market perception.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Layer in review-site sentiment. Auto-generated category names are often unsearchable nonsense ("Revenue Intelligence Cloud Platform"); validate against Google search volume and analyst categories. Messaging hierarchies will drift if regenerated each cycle — maintain the hierarchy in version control and amend, don't recreate.

failure-mode.description: Agents over-cite Dunford / Crossing the Chasm / Ries-Trout without distinguishing when each applies.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Crossing the Chasm is for early-market enterprise tech, not consumer SaaS. Translation/localization: positioning that works in English often fails when literally translated — re-do the canvas per language market.
